buggy_code:
from sklearn.linear_model import Ridge
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
import numpy as np

class MomentumModel:
    def __init__(self):
        self.model = Ridge(alpha=1.0)
        self.scaler = StandardScaler()
        self.is_trained = False

    def train(self, X_train, y_train):
        """Call once before backtesting begins."""
        X_scaled = self.scaler.fit_transform(X_train)
        self.model.fit(X_scaled, y_train)
        self.is_trained = True

    def predict(self, X):
        """Call during backtesting — inference only, no training."""
        if not self.is_trained:
            raise RuntimeError('Model must be trained before predicting')
        X_scaled = self.scaler.transform(X)
        return self.model.predict(X_scaled)

model = MomentumModel()
model.train(X_train, y_train)
predictions = model.predict(X_test)
description: Clean example — model training and inference cleanly separated into distinct methods.
explanation: train() calls fit_transform() on training data only. predict() calls transform() only — no fitting. Guard raises error if predict() called before training. No training inside inference loop.
